stories.title: Europe fears Putin’s ‘window of opportunity’ is now
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — European governments worry Vladimir Putin will see this as his moment to strike. Defense officials and lawmakers fear the Kremlin will consider the next year or two, while Donald Trump is still in the White House and the EU hasn’t yet reinforced its military capacity, as the time to test the West’s commitment to NATO, according to three EU politicians with direct knowledge of the discussions, interviewed for this article. While Russia’s war in Ukraine has shown the l

stories.title: Zelenskyy hints Ukrainian drones could menace Putin’s Victory Day parade
stories.summary: KYIV — Russia’s May 9 Victory Day parade could have some unwelcome guests this year: Ukrainian drones. The 2026 edition of the annual commemoration of Nazi Germany’s defeat at the hands of the Soviet Union was already shaping up to be a less-than-stellar event. Last month, the Kremlin announced that, for the first time in two decades, no tanks or missiles would take part in a parade that usually aims to highlight Moscow’s military might. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: US denies report that warship trying to pass through strait of Hormuz was hit by Iran
stories.summary: US Central Command denies that a ship has been struck after Iran’s Fars news agency claimed two missiles hit a US navy frigate Trump says US navy will ‘guide’ trapped ships from strait of Hormuz amid ‘very positive’ talks with Iran We have a bit more of the statement from Maj Gen Ali Abdollahi, the commander of Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya central headquarters, who said earlier that the US or any other foreign armed forces would be attacked if they entered the strait of Hormuz (see post at 07.39 for

stories.title: Czech competitor wins European Yo-Yo Championship in Bratislava
stories.summary: Jakub Dolejs of the Czech Republic claimed the top title in the main 1A category at the European Yo-Yo Championship in Slovakia.

stories.title: Ukrainian drones hit Russia's Primorsk oil terminal
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Kyiv also hit two of Russia's shadow fleet tankers near the Black Sea port of Novorossiysk. Two sides have continued to fire hundreds of drones at each other on a daily basis.

stories.title: Ukrán drón csapódott a magyar nagykövetséghez közeli felhőkarcolóba Moszkvában
stories.summary: A luxuslakásokkal teli Moszfilm-torony alig hét kilométerre áll a Vörös tértől.

stories.title: Arany János egy műve és a létösszegző versek közül választhatnak a diákok a középszintű magyarérettségi második felében
stories.summary: A diákok az érettségi első részében egy interjút kaptak, amiben a Magyar Kultúra főszerkesztője, Bonczidai Éva kérdezte Szilágyi Márton irodalomtörténészt Jókai Mórról.

stories.title: Rúzsa Magdi, Co Lee és Blaize is Artisjus-díjat kapott
stories.summary: Rúzsa Magdi a legjobb könnyűzenei szövegíró lett, junior kategóriában Gerlóczy Zsigmondot és Kökény Dánielt díjazták.

stories.title: Magyarország legkisebb fejű bohócaival akartam meghekkelni a mesterséges intelligenciát, de megafonos propagandistákat hallucinált tőle
stories.summary: Az MI-t sokkal könnyebb átverni, mint a keresőmotorok régebbi algoritmusait, ami teljesen új keresőoptimalizálási stratégiákhoz és vicces sztorikhoz vezet. Én is kipróbáltam ezt, de csak a nagy nyelvi modellek alapvető problémáihoz jutottam vissza.

stories.title: Magyar Péter az MTA-n: Kialakítjuk azt a kutatóhálózati modellt, ami hosszú távon biztosítja a működést
stories.summary: A leendő miniszterelnök az MTA 200. ünnepi közgyűlésén beszélt, azt mondta: a magyar állam minden évben kétszer-háromszor annyi pénzt költött el propagandára, mint tudományra.

stories.title: “Either everything will change for the better, or we’ll be fired”— how those in Fidesz’s hinterland feel about the election
stories.summary: Grim-faced middle managers, ministry employees hoping for jobs at NER-affiliated companies, sudden turncoats and hopes of a freer atmosphere – in the wake of Hungary's recent election, uncertainty reigns in both the civil service and at state-owned companies.

stories.title: A Fidesz még mindig nem érti
stories.summary: Magyar Péter megint minden rekordot porrá zúzott a Lájkbajnokságban, a Fidesz pedig teljesen lefagyott. Kivéve Pócs Jánost és Deutsch Tamást, de abban sincs sok köszönet. Vajon hányszor kell kimondani, hogy „megújulás”, hogy bárki elhiggye, hogy az tényleg meg is történt?

stories.title: Amíg számított, nem tudták leszedni az uszító plakátokat, most rendeletet írnak rá Szegeden
stories.summary: Megtiltanák a gyűlöletre vagy erőszakra buzdító plakátokat. Korábban még arról volt szó, hogy nincs hatalmuk a magántulajdonú hirdetőfelületek felett, és úgy tűnik, tényleg lehetnek jogi akadályok.

stories.title: Választási vereség ide vagy oda, idén is misével ünneplik Orbán Viktor születésnapját
stories.summary: A leköszönő miniszterelnök május 31-én lesz 63 éves.

stories.title: A Készenléti Rendőrség védi Magyar Pétert a hivatalba lépése után
stories.summary: Az Orbán-kormányok idején a TEK védte a köztársasági elnököt, a miniszterelnököt, a külügyminisztert és a legfőbb ügyészt.

stories.title: Minden eddigi rekordot megdöntve a Telex nyerte a választás hónapját
stories.summary: A rendszerváltás hónapjában a magyar közönség a Telex YouTube-csatornáját kereste fel legtöbbször, a Telex.hu pedig a legolvasottabb híroldal lett áprilisban.

stories.summary: A Telexen Kovács Péter, a Budapesti Fazekas Mihály Gyakorló Általános Iskola és Gimnázium tanára ismertette a feladatokat.

stories.title: Telex breaks all previous records in month of election
stories.summary: Telex’s YouTube channel recorded the highest number of views among Hungary’s online news outlets in April, and Telex.hu was the country's most-read news site during the same time.

stories.title: Fico személyesen mondta el Zelenszkijnek, hogy támogatja Ukrajna EU-csatlakozását
stories.summary: Először találkozott a két vezető Orbán Viktor választási veresége óta.

stories.title: Leálltak a magyar bankrendszer nagyobb összegű átutalásai, késhetnek a fizetések is
stories.summary: Május 4-én arra lettek figyelmesek a hazai bankok és a fizetési cégek, hogy nem mennek át a 20 millió forintnál nagyobb utalások. Mindez sok munkavállaló esetében a fizetések csúszását okozhatja.

stories.title: Eltűnt egy olasz zenész Budapesten, az Interpol is keresi
stories.summary: Az Oggetto Sconosciuto punkegyüttes dobosa május 1-jén tűnt el.

stories.title: Pope appoints former undocumented immigrant as bishop of West Virginia
stories.summary: Leo, who has criticized Trump’s hardline immigration policy, selected Evelio Menjivar-Ayala as state’s new bishop Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Pope Leo XIV has appointed a man who had once entered the United States as an undocumented immigrant, hidden in the trunk of a car, as the new bishop of West Virginia . The pope approved the resignation of Bishop Mark E Brennan of Wheeling-Charleston, West Virginia, and selected Bishop Evelio Menjivar-Ayala

stories.title: Trump claims hostilities in Iran 'have terminated' in letter sent to top US lawmakers
stories.summary: Trump sidestepped a legal deadline for gaining approval from members of Congress over his war in Iran as he sends a letter to top lawmakers claiming that hostilities have ended due to the ongoing ceasefire.

stories.title: The missing Ukrainian reporter, the Russian prison – and a vital lesson learned about journalism in a dangerous age | Laurent Richard
stories.summary: To do our job, investigative journalists are learning to work across borders. This isn’t just about the truth, it’s about staying alive Laurent Richard is a journalist and the director of the Forbidden Stories consortium In February 2025, the body of journalist Viktoriia Roshchyna was finally returned to Ukraine after months of uncertainty. She was one of 757 Ukrainian casualties handed over by Russian authorities as part of an exchange of prisoners and the dead. Roshchyna had disappeared in the

stories.title: Hungary: What will become of Orban and his system?
stories.summary: Outgoing Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban and his Fidesz party are still reeling from their election defeat earlier this month. There is much speculation about what their political future looks like.

stories.title: EU trade chief to meet with Trump’s top trade official as tensions spike
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič will meet with U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer on Tuesday in Paris, as transatlantic trade tensions spike over Washington’s latest threats to impose steep tariffs on European cars. A member of Šefčovič’s cabinet confirmed that the meeting would take place on the eve of a meeting of G7 trade ministers in the French capital on Wednesday. The meeting comes after U.S. President Donald Trump threatened last week to hike tariffs on cars and

stories.title: Japan sees largest protest in support of pacifist constitution as PM Takaichi pushes revisions
stories.summary: Japanese leader Sanae Takaichi has called for discussions to revise the constitution, saying it should ‘reflect the demands of the times’ Japan’s prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, has called for “advanced discussions” on revising the pacifist constitution, as large demonstrations were held nationwide to oppose any changes to the country’s supreme law. Speaking during an official visit to Vietnam, Takaichi said the constitution, which was written by US occupation forces after the second world war,

stories.summary: US president’s post follows flurry of mixed signals including concern Tehran had not ‘paid big enough price’. Plus, could Santa Marta climate talks mark ground zero in push to ditch fossil fuels? Don’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up here Good morning. He made the remark in public comments during the plenary session at the European Political Community summit in Yerevan in Armenia. And both of those are impacting all of us in a very material way. In the United Kingdom, if you look at the economic forecast now and compare it to the

stories.title: Germany news: Merz plays down US military withdrawal plans
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has said a US threat to pull troops out of Germany is "nothing new." Meanwhile, Germany’s interior minister says border controls will stay despite falling asylum numbers. DW has more.

stories.title: Kallas: US troop withdrawal ‘comes as a surprise’
stories.summary: EU top diplomat Kaja Kallas said the U.S. decision to withdraw thousands of troops from Germany had caught European leaders off guard. Speaking on the sidelines of the European Political Community summit in Yerevan, Armenia, Kallas said: “There has been talk about withdrawing U.S. troops from Europe for a long time. But of course, the timing of this announcement comes as a surprise.” Her remarks came after reports that the Pentagon is planning to withdraw around 5,000 troops from Germany over th

stories.title: Austria expels 3 Russian diplomats over alleged espionage
stories.summary: Vienna has expelled three Russian diplomats over alleged espionage, Austria’s Foreign Ministry confirmed Monday. According to Austrian authorities, the officials in question are suspected of carrying out espionage operations using satellite equipment installed on the rooftops of Russian diplomatic buildings in Vienna. In mid-April, Russian Ambassador to Vienna Andrey Yuryevich Grozov was summoned to Austria’s Foreign Ministry and asked to waive the officials’ immunity. After Moscow d

stories.title: Commission blocks EU funding for Huawei solar tech
stories.summary: The European Commission is blocking EU funding for solar panel inverters from what it considers high-risk vendors like China’s tech giant Huawei, it confirmed on Monday. Commission spokesperson Siobhan McGarry told reporters the EU executive has decided to take “concrete action right now” against the “risk of disruption of the EU’s critical infrastructure by foreign actors,” including by “developing guidance on restricting the use of EU funds for projects involving inverters from hig

stories.title: The centre left is not dead. A progressive new counter-Trumpian movement is on the way | Florian Ranft
stories.summary: Social democrats are at last facing up to the failures of globalisation to create equality or deliver for workers If Donald Trump represents the backlash against the liberal rules-based order, then we may now be seeing the backlash to the backlash. In a recent speech , the Spanish prime minister, Pedro Sánchez, spoke of just that. “They scream and shout not because they are winning, but because they know their time is running out,” he said , of those seeking to undermine international law and no

stories.title: Security or justice? Syria faces post-Assad reckoning after string of arrests
stories.summary: New government accused of ‘performative justice’ and making deals with suspects in 2013 Tadamon massacres Ahmad al-Homsi is a deep sleeper, but when he was woken last month and told that Amjad Youssef, a Syrian intelligence officer who killed civilians in the 2013 Tadamon massacres, had been arrested, he bolted out of bed. He ran into the street to find other people already celebrating the news. “We stayed out for almost three or four days celebrating. People from neighbouring areas sent camels,

stories.title: Sketches of Spain at arms: Sim, the anarchist illustrator who drew the civil war from the frontline
stories.summary: José Luis Rey Vila’s powerful sketches of street battles and wearied soldiers brought the conflict to the world. A new show in Barcelona celebrates his overlooked legacy Pablo Picasso may be the artist most famously associated with the Spanish civil war, but as the rifles fired in revolutionary Catalonia, it was those on the frontlines who first captured the conflict. One of the most important was José Luis Rey Vila, whose sketches brought the rhythms of war to life in bold, blocky lines with sp

stories.title: Flaws in Kenya’s AI-driven health reforms driving up costs for the poorest
stories.summary: Exclusive : amid unrest, President William Ruto promised to give all Kenyans access to healthcare. But the algorithm favours the rich, an investigation has found An AI system used to predict how much Kenyans can afford to pay for access to healthcare, has systemically driven up costs for the poor, an investigation has found. The healthcare system being rolled out across the country, a key electoral promise of President William Ruto, was launched in October 2024 and intended to replace Kenya’s de

stories.title: Australia and Japan commit to ‘support the flow’ of energy – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Jim Chalmers says next Tuesday’s budget will not extend the 26 cent fuel tax cut beyond June. But with Australia “hostage” to the wild swings in global oil prices amid the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran, the treasurer said there was “a range of contingencies” prepared to help support households and the economy from any escalating damage as a result of the Middle East conflict. stories.title: Iran says ships being ‘guided’ by US through Hormuz must work with its armed forces
stories.summary: Shipowners wary as Iran says it will attack any foreign military entering waterway under Trump plan Middle East crisis – live updates The US has launched an operation to “guide” ships trapped in the Gulf by the Iran war through a southern route of the strait of Hormuz, even as Tehran insists that any such transits will have to be coordinated with its armed forces. The scheme was announced as “Project Freedom” on Sunday night by Donald Trump on his social media page, where he portrayed it as a hu

stories.title: Europe will not submit to an ‘insular and brutal world’, says Carney
stories.summary: Speaking at meeting of European Political Community, Canadian PM says gathering points to a better path forward Europe will not submit to a more “brutal world”, and can instead be the base from which a new international order can be rebuilt, Mark Carney, the Canadian prime minister, has said. Carney was speaking as the first non-European leader to attend a meeting of the European Political Community, which opened on Monday amid high tensions in the strait of Hormuz and renewed doubts about the U

stories.title: Republicans split with Trump and back Haitians – to save their seats
stories.summary: In Ohio, where Haitians have helped revive the economy, vulnerable Republicans are reluctant to toe the Trump line For months Carl Ruby, a pastor at a church in Springfield, Ohio, and a prominent supporter of the city’s estimated 10,000 Haitian immigrants, had been trying to contact his local congressman, Republican Mike Turner. Ruby had long hoped for an opportunity to explain in person the difficulties facing Haitians in Springfield and how that community had helped revive the struggling town.

stories.title: Labor extends EV tax break to encourage cheaper vehicles amid soaring fuel prices
stories.summary: As the cost of the scheme blows out, the government has announced the full discount will be retained for another year, after which it will only apply to EVs costing less than $75,000 Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor has opted to retain its electric vehicle discount in full for another year, as Australians rush to buy EVs amid soaring fuel costs linked to the Iran war. But the budget next Tuesday will include a number of “sensible changes” that wind back the scal

stories.title: Europe must face up to ‘tensions’ with Trump, Keir Starmer says
stories.summary: Comments at European Political Community summit come as Britain seeks to join EU’s loan scheme for Ukraine UK politics live – latest updates Keir Starmer has acknowledged that “tensions” between Donald Trump and Europe are high as he attends a summit of the European Political Community dominated by the conflicts in Ukraine and Iran. With European leaders concerned over the US president’s waning interest in the Ukraine war, the prime minister will use the summit in Armenia to begin negotiations t

stories.title: Reform government could cause Truss-style chaos, says renewables industry
stories.summary: Lobbyist Tara Singh says stripping projects of subsidy contracts would undermine investor confidence in UK Britain could be beset by levels of economic chaos last seen under Liz Truss if a Reform UK government were to fulfil its promise to strip renewable energy projects of subsidy contracts, according to the industry’s chief lobbyist. The anti-renewables policy put forward by Nigel Farage’s populist party would severely undermine investor confidence in the energy industry and across the wider U

stories.title: Driver injured after truck was struck by United plane landing at Newark airport
stories.summary: Delivery truck driver emerged with minor injuries after underside of jet hit tractor trailer in New Jersey on Sunday The driver of a delivery truck emerged with only minor injuries after his tractor trailer was struck by a passenger jet landing in New Jersey ’s busy Newark airport on Sunday afternoon, officials said. New Jersey state police said a landing tire and the underside of a United Airlines plane arriving from Venice, Italy, hit the truck in question. The Boeing 767 aircraft also clipped

stories.title: Iran war: US says both military and merchant ships have passed through Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump had earlier announced the "Project Freedom" operation to "help free up" ships stuck in the key waterway. Iran said that would amount to a truce violation. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Tehran regime executes three protesters, one 'without evidence'
stories.summary: Iran’s judiciary says three protesters detained over the January unrest in the city of Mashhad have been hanged, as rights groups report four murder convicts were executed in a unrelated case in Isfahan.

stories.title: Nigeria plans to repatriate nationals willing to leave South Africa after attacks
stories.summary: There has been a wave of anti-migrant protests in South Africa, some of which have turned violent.

stories.title: Rudy Giuliani hospitalized in ‘critical but stable condition,’ spokesperson says
stories.summary: Former New York mayor and key Trump ally Rudy Giuliani has been hospitalized, a spokesperson announced Sunday on X. Giuliani, who will turn 82 later this month, is in critical but stable condition,  spokesperson Ted Goodman wrote . President Donald Trump blamed his condition on the Democratic opponents Giuliani accrued while working as Trump’s personal lawyer. “What a tragedy that he was treated so badly by the Radical Left Lunatics, Democrats ALL — AND HE WAS RIGHT ABOUT EVERYTHING!” Trump

stories.title: MEP hits back at ‘fake’ octopus dish served in EU Parliament canteen
stories.summary: Don’t cook polbo á feira if you don’t know how to do it. Spanish center-right MEP Adrián Vázquez Lázara is hosting a traditional Galician dinner Wednesday evening to defend the region’s role as “Europe’s leading fishing power,” the lawmaker told POLITICO in a statement. Invitations were sent after the European Parliament canteen dared to serve what Vázquez called a “fake” version of Galicia’s most iconic dish: boiled octopus with olive oil, paprika and s

stories.title: Suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship is no reason to ‘panic,’ WHO says
stories.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) said Monday the risk to the wider public “remains low” after a suspected outbreak of a rare and deadly virus killed three people and left three others ill aboard a cruise ship in the Atlantic. The U.N. health agency had previously confirmed one case of hantavirus — a rodent-borne disease that can cause life-threatening respiratory problems — aboard the Dutch cruise ship, and five more cases are suspected. Dutch cruise operator Oceanwide Expeditions

stories.title: UK seeks access to EU’s €5B tech scale-up fund
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The EU and the U.K. will start talks on British access to a new €5 billion fund that wants to provide late-stage funding to tech companies. Both sides have agreed to “commence negotiations,” European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen and U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Monday following a meeting at the European Political Community summit in Armenia. Years after Brexit, the talks are another sign of warming relations between the EU and the U.K., as both sides

stories.title: Rare comet to flash through New Zealand skies – before it disappears for 170,000 years
stories.summary: Over the next two weeks stargazers have a chance to spot the blue-green orb and smudgy tail of comet known as C/2025 R3 PanSTARRS A comet formed on the edges of the solar system will grace southern skies over the next fortnight, giving viewers a rare chance to glimpse it before it disappears from view for another 170,000 years. The comet – known as C/2025 R3 PanSTARRS – had been travelling through the northern hemisphere but has “swung around the sun” and is now visible in the south, said Josh A

stories.title: ‘It’s a world heritage site, but it’s my home’: the last resident of Casa Milà on life in Gaudí’s masterwork
stories.summary: Ana Viladomiu has been a ‘privileged’ resident of the once derided, now revered Barcelona apartment building for almost 40 years Imagine that you live in an enormous, beautiful apartment designed by one of the world’s most admired architects in the most expensive street in Spain and for which you pay a derisory rent, with the right to live there until you die. stories.title: EU forging closer ties with Armenia as it sends experts to help counter Russian interference
stories.summary: Bloc’s leaders to hold first summit with Armenia on Tuesday at it ramps up efforts to combat Kremlin’s influence The EU is sending a team of experts specialised in combating Russian propaganda and interference to Armenia, as it increases its support to the former Soviet republic in a tense political period. stories.title: Could Iran’s escalating economic crisis weaken negotiating position with US?
stories.summary: War has combined with battered economy to leave Tehran wondering how hardline it can afford to be Iran may not be choking like a stuffed pig as Donald Trump predicted, but its economy is in serious difficulty as a combination of a massive war-damages bill, inflation, currency devaluation, unemployment and a contraction in oil revenues combine to leave the political elite worrying how hardline they can afford to be with their US negotiators. stories.title: Wikipedia founder brands Australia’s social media ban an ‘unmitigated disaster’ and ‘embarrassment’

stories.summary: The Olympic Games minister, Tim Mander, and the child safety minister, Amanda Camm say they were not in a relationship when they were sworn in Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Queensland’s opposition has accused the LNP government of being in an “integrity crisis” over an allegedly undisclosed affair between two ministers and has urged the government to show how the pair are avoiding conflicts of interest. stories.title: Coalition accused of secretly giving big tobacco lobbyists private platform in parliament
stories.summary: Exclusive : A Senate committee considering the illegal tobacco trade in Australia hears closed-session evidence from cigarette manufacturers Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anti-smoking campaigners have accused the Coalition of secretly giving tobacco giants access to a parliamentary inquiry, a move they say undermines more than 15 years of precedent to protect public health. On Monday, representatives from tobacco company Philip Morris appeared before a Senate commi

stories.title: France’s Jean-Luc Mélenchon announces 2027 presidential bid
stories.summary: The radical left leader will seek presidency for the fourth time – despite previously vowing to stand aside Jean-Luc Mélenchon, France’s radical left leader, has confirmed he will run again for president next spring, saying it was urgent for the country to stand up against war being waged by the US and Israel in the Middle East. The 74-year-old veteran leader of La France Insoumise (LFI), announced in an interview with the French broadcaster TF1 that he would run for the presidency for the fourt

stories.title: Jeremy Bamber banned from communicating with media from prison
stories.summary: Bamber, 65, has long used press interviews to campaign against convictions for murder of five family members Jeremy Bamber, who has served more than 40 years in prison for murdering five members of his family, has been banned from communicating with the media. Bamber was convicted in 1986 by a 10-2 majority of shooting his adoptive mother and father, his sister and her six-year-old twins at the parents’ family farmhouse in Essex a year earlier. stories.title: Iran executes three men in relation to January anti-regime protests
stories.summary: Hangings are latest in wave of near-daily killings as authorities seek to instil fear amid war with US and Israel Middle East crisis – live updates Iran has executed three men charged in connection with political protests this January, authorities have said, the latest in a wave of hangings against the backdrop of the war against the US and Israel. stories.title: At least 13 hurt in shooting at Oklahoma campground party
stories.summary: No arrests made, police say, and site of shooting – a heavily wooded area – is slowing search for evidence and suspect At least 13 people were hurt in a mass shooting during a party at a campground in Oklahoma on Sunday night, authorities said. The shooting took place at Spring Creek Park on Arcadia Lake about 15 miles north-east of downtown Oklahoma City, when gunfire erupted during a gathering of young people at about 9pm. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hearing for suspect in White House correspondents’ dinner shooting scheduled for today – US politics live
stories.summary: Cole Allen remains in custody after being charged with attempting to assassinate Donald Trump Sign up for the Breaking News US email The new nominee for US surgeon general is an “effective communicator” who appears to be “mainstream enough” to pass confirmation before the US Senate, experts say. stories.summary: Officials say they will not authorise docking ‘to protect public health’ after deaths of three passengers What is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship? Officials in Cape Verde have said they will not allow a cruise ship believed to be harbouring an outbreak of a rare respiratory virus to dock in its ports, after three passengers died. The statement on Monday came hours after global health officials said they were scrambling to investigate the suspected outbreak o

stories.title: Vine video-sharing app is back – and battling AI slop
stories.summary: Twitter co-founder Jack Dorsey backs launch of Vine reboot, Divine, where content must be made by a human As a pioneer of the short-form video format, Vine has been credited as one of the most influential – if short-lived – social media platforms. The app, which allowed users to record a looping six seconds of video, boomed in popularity after its launch in 2013, creating a plethora of viral comedy sketches and internet memes. It hit 100 million monthly active users at its peak and helped launch

stories.title: Canada to be first non-European nation at EPC summit as Carney seeks allies
stories.summary: Attendance at European Political Community meeting in Yerevan seen as part of effort to build ties after US rupture Canada is to become the first non-European country to attend a meeting of the European Political Community when the prime minister, Mark Carney, joins Monday’s summit of the 48-plus nation grouping in Yerevan, Armenia. Carney has said he is determined to build a new network of trade and diplomatic alliances after the loss of US markets under Donald Trump. His presence will also rep

stories.title: Merz ‘not giving up on working with Trump’ despite Iran war spat
stories.summary: German chancellor downplays US military drawbacks and president’s barbs in TV interview The German chancellor, Friedrich Merz, has said he will not give up on working with the US president, Donald Trump, despite a spat between the leaders over the war in Iran. “I am not giving up on working on the transatlantic relationship,” Merz told the public broadcaster ARD in an interview due to air on Sunday night. “Nor am I giving up on working with Donald Trump.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Three passengers dead after suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
stories.summary: Dutch husband and wife and third unidentified person reported to have died, with three further people taken ill What is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship? A suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus infection on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean killed three people – including an elderly married couple – and sickened at least three others, the World Health Organisation (WHO) and South Africa’s health department said on Sunday. The WHO said an investigation w

stories.title: Ex-New York Mayor Giuliani in hospital, condition 'critical'
stories.summary: Rudy Giuliani was dubbed "America’s mayor" for leading New York City through the aftermath of the 9/11 terror attacks in 2001. But his reputation took a deep dive amid his support for Trump in recent years.

stories.title: US transfers crew of seized Iranian ship to Pakistan
stories.summary: Pakistan's Foreign Ministry said that the "confidence-building measure" was supported by both sides.

stories.title: Women's Champions League: Bayern fall to Barcelona in semis
stories.summary: Bayern Munich have come up short in their bid to win the Women's Champions League for the first time after losing to Barcelona in the semifinals.

stories.title: Angine de Poitrine: A masked rock band conquers the internet
stories.summary: From underground experiment to sold-out international concerts within days — as the French-Canadian band Angine de Poitrine starts touring the world, we take a look at the viral phenomenon.

stories.title: EPC summit: Europe has 'gotten the message' from Trump
stories.summary: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said at the opening of the EPC summit in Armenia that Europe has "heard" Washington's frustration over its conflict with Iran and are "stepping up." DW has the latest.

stories.title: Georg Baselitz, artist renowned for his upside-down paintings, dies
stories.summary: He was thrown out of art school at the age of 18 because of his love for Picasso. The German artist who never lost his rebellious streak has died at the age of 88.

stories.title: Decoding China: Beijing courts Europe amid US dysfunction
stories.summary: China is hoping to secure new allies for a future multipolar world order as the partnership between the US and the European powers slides into a crisis.

stories.title: India news: Modi claims victory in West Bengal election
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's Hindu nationalist BJP party is poised to govern the state for the first time ever. Modi hopes to make inroads into other opposition strongholds too. Follow live.

stories.title: How child soldiers in Sudan become influencers on TikTok
stories.summary: Some Sudanese child soldiers have social media accounts are celebrated by millions of followers. Psychologists and other experts warn this is a dangerous trend.

stories.title: Antisemitism 'allowed to come into the open' says Bondi victim's daughter
stories.summary: Sheina Gutnick was the first person to give evidence at the royal commission investigating the attack.

stories.title: BBC uncovers the scammers exploiting dogs to elicit donations from animal lovers

stories.title: Oil prices edge up despite Trump vowing action in Hormuz tensions

stories.title: Former New York City mayor Rudy Giuliani in critical condition in hospital
stories.summary: The long-time champion of Trump and former New York mayor is in hospital, according to his spokesman.

stories.title: Participants compete to lure worms from the ground at quirky UK festival
stories.summary: Contestants gathered in southern England for the annual “Wormcharming” competition, attempting to coax worms from the ground without digging.

stories.summary: The attack comes as the Kremlin prepares to hold a scaled-back military parade on Saturday.

stories.title: Humanoid robots deployed to direct traffic in China
stories.summary: Humanoid robots have been deployed at intersections in the Chinese city of Hangzhou to assist traffic police and guide pedestrians.

stories.title: Three dead in suspected virus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
stories.summary: The operator of the MV Hondius ship says a Dutch husband and wife, as well as a German national, have died but the cause has not yet been established.

stories.title: New alliances shakes up Nigerian political landscape
stories.summary: Peter Obi and Rabiu Kwankwaso, who came third and fourth respectively in the last elections, switch parties.

stories.title: Iran executes detainee of the 2022 'Woman, Life, Freedom' movement
stories.summary: Iran's judiciary announced on Sunday that it had carried out the death execution of Mehrab Abdollahzadeh, a man arrested during 2022 protest movement, launched after the death of 22-year-old Mahsa Amini while in Iran's morality police custody.

stories.title: Seven killed in southern Lebanon amid Israeli airstrikes and ceasefire tensions
stories.summary: State media reported that several villages were hit as neighbourhoods near the border are being levelled. The IDF maintains that 50 airstrikes conducted over 24 hours specifically targeted Hezbollah infrastructure.

stories.title: Russian strikes kill 10 as Zelensky says Ukraine hits oil tankers and terminal
stories.summary: Fatalities have been reported around Ukraine as Kyiv says Russian "shadow fleet" tankers were hit by drones.

stories.title: Merz 'not giving up' on Germany's relationship with US
stories.summary: The German chancellor said there was "no connection" between disagreements over the Iran war and the pullout of US troops from Germany. He insisted he shared Trump's goal of ensuring Iran never obtains a nuclear weapon.

stories.title: Suspected Hantavirus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship kills 3, WHO says
stories.summary: The virus usually spreads by contact with the urine or feces of infected rodents like rats and mice. It can cause a severe and sometimes deadly lung infection.

stories.title: Massive crowds fill Rio's Copacabana beach for free Shakira concert
stories.summary: Latin pop queen Shakira kicks off her free mega-concert on Rio's famed Copacabana beach under a full moon, with drones forming the image of a she-wolf -- her nickname -- overhead as she takes the stage over an hour late.

stories.title: Concern for jailed Iranian Nobel laureate as brother fears she is dying
stories.summary: Her family said the 54-year-old had been taken from prison to a local hospital after a sharp deterioration in her health.

stories.title: Police officer lowered into crocodile-infested river to recover human remains
stories.summary: Police suspect the human remains belong to a businessman swept away by floodwater last week.

stories.title: Two US service members reported missing in Morocco, officials say
stories.summary: A search and rescue operation is underway for the service members, who are believed to have been involved in an accident, according to authorities.

stories.title: Who shot a Secret Service officer at the Trump press dinner?
stories.summary: Court papers do not explicitly accuse the suspect of shooting the officer, even as officials say it was not "friendly fire".

stories.title: Massive sea lion “Chonkers” draws crowds in San Francisco

stories.title: Amsterdam bans public adverts for meat and fossil fuels

stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök szerint a „Project Freedom” közel-keleti idő szerint hétfő reggel kezdődött el.

stories.summary: Szerdán már több helyen fordul elő zápor, zivatar.

stories.summary: Irán kijelentette, hogy a tengeri szoros biztonsága az ő kezükben van, és nem kérnek Trump felszabadító műveletéből.

stories.summary: A tavalyihoz hasonlóan idén is a Mi Hazánk tartott május elsejei rendezvényt a parkban.

stories.summary: A hétfői tárgyaláson Herényi Károlyt és Donáth Annát is meghallgatták. Mindannyian úgy vélik, politikailag motivált az ügy.

Az igazságügyiminiszter-jelöltet ért kritikákról a Tisza frakcióvezetője azt mondta: kritikához mindenkinek joga van. A Fidesz volt kampányigazgatója pedig nem érzi úgy, hogy hazudtak volna a kampányban.

stories.summary: How much Donald Trump can do with the time he has left as president will depend massively on the cost of living.

stories.title: Could Santa Marta climate talks mark ground zero in push to ditch fossil fuels?
stories.summary: Colombia hosted nearly 60 countries at pivotal time on world stage for fight to transition to a clean energy future Looking out to sea from the grey sandy beaches of Santa Marta, on Colombia’s Caribbean coast, it is never hard to spot evidence of the country’s thriving fossil fuel export trade. Oil tankers ride at anchor on the horizon and sometimes, locals say, lumps of coal wash up on the shore, blown off the collier ships that carry cargos from the nearby mines. It was here, on Wednesday even

stories.title: Ukraine plans army pay hikes, phased discharge — Zelenskyy
stories.summary: The Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced reforms to boost morale and address military complaints about exhaustion and uneven workloads on the frontline.

stories.title: Iran executes 21-year-old karate champion as UN warns of death penalty surge
stories.summary: Tehran executed 21-year-old karate athlete Sassan Azadvar early Thursday after protest arrest, as UN and rights groups warn of a surge in executions and mass detentions since the start of the Iran war.

stories.title: Iran war impact on shipping affecting refugee aid with freight rates soaring, UNHCR says
stories.summary: The agency has re-routed sea cargo via Jordan's Aqaba Red Sea port and has switched to land corridors including truck routes from Dubai across the Arabian peninsula and Turkey.

stories.title: Cuba condemns new US sanctions as 'illegal' and 'abusive'
stories.summary: The new measures come on top of a US blockade of oil to Cuba that has caused widespread blackouts and fuel shortages.

stories.title: Trump follows through on threats as he announces significant troop withdrawal from Germany
stories.summary: Trump had earlier threatened to pull some US troops from Germany after Chancellor Friedrich Merz expressed his disapproval with the US-Israeli war on Iran, which has upended the Middle East and caused a global oil price crisis.

stories.title: Trump jokes that the US Navy will takeover Cuba 'almost immediately' on the way back from Iran
stories.summary: Trump appeared to hint at a potential military operation in Cuba after US forces “finish the job” in Iran. The comments come as Washington announced a set of new sanctions targeting the island nation on Friday, which Havana slammed as a form of collective punishment.

stories.title: CCTV captures moment man attacks nun in Jerusalem – video
stories.summary: Israeli police released a video on Thursday showing an attack on a French Catholic nun in Jerusalem. The footage shows the man running up behind the nun and pushing her to the ground. After briefly walking away, the attacker, who appears to be Jewish, returns to kick the nun as she lies on the ground and only stops when a passerby intervenes. Police said they had arrested a 36-year-old man. The date when the video was recorded was verified by an official statement from the Israeli foreign minist

stories.title: German museum to return rare Irritator dinosaur skull to Brazil
stories.summary: Spinosaurid fossil bought by Stuttgart institution in 1991 has been the subject of a long restitution campaign It is a 113-million-year-old bone of contention. After Stuttgart’s museum of natural history bought a fossilised dinosaur skull in 1991, researchers found it was the most complete spinosaurid skull known to date, belonging to a previously unknown genus of the huge meat-eating dinosaurs. Continue reading...

stories.title: Zambia cancels world’s largest human rights and tech summit days before start
stories.summary: Government blocks RightsCon 2026 conference saying it did not ‘align with national values’ The world’s largest conference on human rights and technology has been cancelled just days before it was due to start after the Zambian government told organisers it did not align with “national values”. Zambia’s government had originally welcomed the RightsCon 2026 summit on “human rights in the digital age”, due to be held in the capital, Lusaka, on 5-8 May, but Thabo Kawana, permanent secretary for the

stories.title: Germany needs no tips from Donald Trump, says Merz's deputy
stories.summary: Germany's vice chancellor says US President Donald Trump has made a mess of the Iran war and that his focus should be on ending it. As fuel prices soar, Lars Klingbeil said Trump bears responsibility for the fallout.

stories.title: Kosovo to hold snap elections in June
stories.summary: Kosovo will soon hold its third parliamentary election in a year and a half. Prime Minister Albin Kurti was able to form a government in February, but now there's political deadlock over who should be elected president.

stories.title: How the Iran war is hurting travelers, airline industry
stories.summary: Europeans may have to reconsider their summer travel plans, as a jet fuel shortage triggered by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz could lead airlines to set higher ticket prices.

stories.title: Alex Zanardi, F1 driver and multiple CART and Paralympic champion, dies at 59
stories.summary: Italian race driver turned Paralympian Alex Zanardi has died aged 59. The former Formula 1 driver and two-time CART champion lost his legs in a horrific crash at the peak of his powers, but his drive to compete endured.

stories.title: King Charles III wraps up US state visit with Arlington tribute
stories.summary: King Charles III concluded his four-day US state visit with appearances in Virginia, a tribute at Arlington National Cemetery and a farewell ceremony before departing from Maryland.

stories.title: Kiwi birds return to New Zealand's capital after a century away
stories.summary: Wellington has welcomed its 250th relocated kiwi as the Capital Kiwi Project works to restore New Zealand's national bird to hills where it has been absent for over a century.

stories.title: Israel arrests man filmed attacking Catholic nun near Jerusalem’s Old City
stories.summary: Israeli police arrested a 36-year-old man who was filmed assaulting a French Catholic nun near Jerusalem’s Old City, in what it said was "zero tolerance to all acts of violence".

stories.title: Brixen Water Light Festival 2026 puts peace at its centre
stories.summary: The Brixen Water Light Festival 2026 runs to 16 May in South Tyrol, with 24 installations from 13 countries exploring peace against a Dolomites backdrop.

stories.title: The world wants to eliminate cervical cancer - how Australian scientists led the way
stories.summary: For the first time, Australia has recorded no new cervical cancer diagnoses in women under 25. To get there, they vaccinated young men.

stories.title: Russia launches new Soyuz-5 rocket from Kazakhstan cosmodrome in first test flight
stories.summary: On Thursday, April 30, at 23:00 local time (Astana), the Baikonur Cosmodrome witnessed the first test launch of the Soyuz-5/Sunkar vehicle. The rocket lifted off from Site 45 as part of flight development tests, according to Kazakhstan’s Ministry of AI and Digital Development.

stories.title: US President Donald Trump says 'not satisfied' with new peace proposal from Iran
stories.summary: Meanwhile, Washington is gripped by a legalistic debate over whether Trump has passed a deadline for requesting congressional approval for the war.

stories.title: Thousands of 'lost Canadians' have applied for dual citizenship - is Canada ready?
stories.summary: Changes to Canadian citizenship rules have led to thousands of new applications - many coming from the US.

stories.title: Trump claims other presidents flouted war powers law. It's a mixed record
stories.summary: Both Bushes and Reagan won authorisation for wars, but Obama and Clinton ducked the requirement.

stories.title: 'This tree was planted by my ancestor hundreds of years ago and my family settled here'
stories.summary: Oral history traces a family's roots to a tree planted in the 13th Century in a Ghanaian fishing town.

stories.title: 'If we sleep, they bite': Rats and weasels infest camps for displaced Gazans
stories.summary: In the Gaza Strip, the daily battles are now with rats, weasels, and other pests spreading diseases.

stories.title: Spirit Airlines says it is going out of business and ending operations 'immediately' after 34 years
stories.summary: The budget airline had hoped for a government bailout to arrive on Friday to prevent it from going out of business. The Trump administration announced on Friday that it had offered the airline its final proposal, but a deal could not be reached.

stories.title: Turkish police fire tear gas and arrest at least 570 people at May Day rallies
stories.summary: Earlier this week, authorities issued arrest warrants for 62 people, of whom they deemed 46, including journalists, trade unionists and opposition figures, were "likely to carry out attacks."

stories.title: Afghanistan says Pakistani strikes kill seven and wound 85 in first attack since peace talks
stories.summary: Pakistan officials dismiss Afghan media reports and official statements about strikes on university in Kunar province as ‘blatant lie’ Mortars and missiles fired from Pakistan on Monday struck a university and civilian homes in north-eastern Afghanistan, killing seven people and wounding at least 85, Afghan officials said. Pakistan denied the accusation of targeting a university. Continue reading...

stories.title: Sri Lanka police arrest 22 Buddhist monks after 110kg of cannabis found in luggage
stories.summary: Customs officials say group allegedly hid 5kg of ‘kush’ in false walls of bags on return from Bangkok holiday Twenty-two Buddhist monks are in Sri Lankan police custody after customs officials found 110kg of high-grade cannabis concealed in their luggage, the largest ever drug bust at Colombo’s main international airport. The group, mostly junior monks in training from temples across Sri Lanka, were alleged to have “carried about five kilos of the narcotic concealed within false walls in their l

stories.title: Germany news: Greens and CDU agree Baden-Württemberg deal
stories.summary: The state of Baden-Württemberg's Greens and conservatives have forged a coalition deal after weeks of talks. Meanwhile, unions are staging May Day rallies to demand better work conditions and a wealth tax.

stories.title: US seeks 'maritime freedom' coalition to restart Strait of Hormuz shipping
stories.summary: Iran effectively closed the crucial waterway when the war broke out on 28 February. Normally one-fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas transits the waterway. Its closure has sent energy prices skyrocketing around the world.
